divergences.description: agent_b and agent_a lean toward stricter pre-release gating for internal docs/governance artifacts, while agent_c keeps most governance docs as urgent-but-deferrable (except SECURITY.md as blocker).

divergences.topic: Audit scope breadth vs execution focus
divergences.description: agent_b favors a broad 19-issue audit surface; agent_a and agent_c favor tighter prioritization to reduce release execution overhead.

agent_b still treats more branding/domain coupling as critical, whereas agent_c narrows blocker/critical status to runtime or deploy-impacting references and treats attribution-style branding as non-blocking.

rationale: agent_b is strongest on completeness, correction depth, and risk framing, and agent_a is strongest on concise, actionable prioritization. All three now converge on core blockers (deployability lock-in, security disclosure policy, licensing clarity), but still differ on how aggressively to gate governance/docs and how broad the critical set should be before release.
